questions.question: Simplify √(36x²). Assume x > 0.
questions.explanation: √(36x²) = √36 · √(x²) = 6 · x = 6x. Since x > 0, we take the positive square root.

questions.question: Solve for x: √x = 7
questions.explanation: Square both sides: (√x)² = 7². This gives x = 49. Check: √49 = 7 ✓

questions.question: Simplify √(50). Give your answer in the form a√b where b has no perfect square factors.
questions.explanation: 50 = 25 · 2, so √50 = √25 · √2 = 5√2. Since 2 has no perfect square factors, this is simplified.

questions.question: Simplify the expression: (12)/(√4). Give a numeric answer.
Solve for x: √(x + 3) = 4
questions.explanation: Square both sides: x + 3 = 16. Subtract 3: x = 13. Check: √(13 + 3) = √16 = 4 ✓
